critic_raw:
## ML Critique Round 1 (Run 2)

**Issue 1 — Temporal leakage via random split:**
24-month search data partitioned by stratified random allocation. Future queries train the model on behavioral patterns not available at inference time. Temporal leakage inflates AUROC. Chronological split required.

**Issue 2 — AUROC for retrieval ranking:**
AUROC treats all impression positions equivalently. In first-stage retrieval, value concentrates in top-10 positions. NDCG@10 is the standard metric for ranking tasks; AUROC obscures whether relevant items appear at rank 5 or rank 25.
defender_raw:
## Defense Round 1 (Run 2)

**Issue 1:** I concede. Random stratified split is inappropriate for time-ordered search data. Temporal leakage is real. Chronological split needed.

**Issue 2:** I concede. AUROC was chosen to handle class imbalance and query-frequency heterogeneity, but for a ranking/retrieval task, position matters. NDCG@10 should be primary. AUROC can be retained as a secondary discrimination metric.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication Multiround (Run 2)

Both issues conceded in Round 1. Critique wins on both counts.

Verdict: critique_wins. Points resolved: 0. Force-resolved: 0. Rate: 0.0
